SEARCH
Package com.hotelreservation.reservation.controller

Class ReservationController

java.lang.Object
com.hotelreservation.reservation.controller.ReservationController
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/reservations") public class ReservationController extends Object
REST controller for reservation management.

Follows the thin-controller principle: all business logic is delegated to ReservationService. This class handles only HTTP concerns.

User identity is supplied via request headers injected by the API Gateway after JWT validation — X-User-Id and X-Username — rather than re-parsing the JWT token in this service.

Access policy:

  • Booking and personal reservation operations — require ROLE_USER or ROLE_ADMIN.
  • Admin-only operations — require ROLE_ADMIN.

    Description

    Constructs the controller with the required reservation service.

  • Constructor Details

    • ReservationController

      public ReservationController(ReservationService reservationService)
      Constructs the controller with the required reservation service.
      Parameters:
      reservationService - service handling all reservation business logic

  • Method Details

    • createReservation

      @PostMapping @PreAuthorize("hasAnyRole(\'USER\', \'ADMIN\')") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ReservationDTO> createReservation(@Valid @RequestBody @Valid ReservationDTO dto, @RequestHeader("X-User-Id") String userId, @RequestHeader("X-Username") String username)
      Books a hotel room for the authenticated user.

      The X-User-Id and X-Username headers are injected automatically by the API Gateway from the validated JWT — the client does not send these directly.

      Parameters:
      dto - the validated reservation request containing roomId and dates
      userId - the authenticated user's ID, forwarded by the API Gateway
      username - the authenticated user's username, forwarded by the API Gateway
      Returns:
      201 Created with the confirmed reservation details

    • getAllReservations

      @GetMapping @PreAuthorize("hasRole(\'ADMIN\')") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<List<ReservationDTO>> getAllReservations()
      Retrieves all reservations in the system. Requires ROLE_ADMIN.
      Returns:
      200 OK with a list of every reservation

    • getMyReservations

      @GetMapping("/my") @PreAuthorize("hasAnyRole(\'USER\', \'ADMIN\')") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<List<ReservationDTO>> getMyReservations(@RequestHeader("X-User-Id") String userId)
      Retrieves all reservations belonging to the currently authenticated user.
      Parameters:
      userId - the authenticated user's ID, forwarded by the API Gateway
      Returns:
      200 OK with the user's reservations ordered by check-in date descending

    • cancelMyReservation

      @DeleteMapping("/my/{reservationId}") @PreAuthorize("hasAnyRole(\'USER\', \'ADMIN\')") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ApiResponse> cancelMyReservation(@PathVariable Long reservationId, @RequestHeader("X-User-Id") String userId)
      Cancels a specific reservation owned by the authenticated user.

      The service layer enforces ownership — a user cannot cancel another user's reservation.

      Parameters:
      reservationId - the ID of the reservation to cancel
      userId - the authenticated user's ID, forwarded by the API Gateway
      Returns:
      200 OK with a success message

    • deleteReservation

      @DeleteMapping("/{reservationId}") @PreAuthorize("hasRole(\'ADMIN\')") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ApiResponse> deleteReservation(@PathVariable Long reservationId)
      Deletes any reservation by ID regardless of ownership. Requires ROLE_ADMIN.
      Parameters:
      reservationId - the ID of the reservation to delete
      Returns:
      200 OK with a success message
